info.title: Audit API
info.description: Retrieve audit events from an Anaplan tenant. Supports filtering by event type and date range. Responses are available in JSON or Common Event Format (CEF) via the `Accept` header. Requires the Tenant Auditor role.

servers.description: Production base URL — Apiary-confirmed and live-tested. The Audit API is served only from this dedicated host; the regional api.anaplan.com data-plane hosts return 404 for /audit/api/1.

get.description:
Retrieves audit events for the tenant associated with the authenticated user. Use `type` to filter by event category. Use `dateFrom`/`dateTo` for a specific date range, or `intervalInHours` for a rolling window; the queried range cannot exceed 30 days. If no date filter is supplied, the previous 30 days are returned. Use `limit` and `offset` for pagination (the documented limit cap of 10000 is not enforced server-side — see the `limit` parameter).

Set `Accept: text/plain` to receive results in Common Event Format (CEF). Default is `application/json`.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Type of events to return. Each value filters to events whose `eventTypeId` carries a matching prefix. Confirmed via live testing: `all` (every event), `user_activity` (`USR-*`), `access_control` (`AUTHZ-*`), `int` (integrations, `INT-*`), `conn_mgmt` (connection / SAML management, `CONN-*`), `comment` (`COMMENT-*`), `byok` (Bring Your Own Key encryption), `plan_iq` (PlanIQ), and `forecaster`. A `workflow` category also exists for the Workflow product but could not be confirmed on the test tenant (product not enabled), so it is omitted from the enum. Note: the enum is not enforced server-side — an unrecognised value is treated as `all` rather than rejected.

parameters.name: dateFrom
parameters.description: Start of the event date range (Unix timestamp in milliseconds, UTC). The span between `dateFrom` and `dateTo` cannot exceed 30 days — a wider range returns 400 `FAILURE_BAD_REQUEST`.

parameters.name: dateTo
parameters.description: End of the event date range (Unix timestamp in milliseconds, UTC). The span between `dateFrom` and `dateTo` cannot exceed 30 days — a wider range returns 400 `FAILURE_BAD_REQUEST`.

parameters.name: intervalInHours
parameters.description: Return events from the previous N hours. For example, `2` returns the last two hours of events. Bound by the same 30-day cap as the date range: a value above `720` (30 days) returns 400 `FAILURE_BAD_REQUEST`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return per page. The documentation states a cap of 10000, but live testing showed the server does not enforce it — larger limits are honored and return more than 10000 records.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Zero-based index of the first result to return. Use with `limit` for pagination.

schema.description: Audit events in Common Event Format (CEF), one event per line. Each line has the form `<ISO-8601 timestamp>  CEF:0|Anaplan, Inc.||null|<eventTypeId>|<message>|<key=value extensions>`, where the extension carries the event fields (`id`, `userId`, `tenantId`, `success`, `additionalAttributes`, ...). Unlike the JSON response, the CEF output is not paginated: it returns every event in the requested window, ignores `limit`/`offset`, and has no `meta`/paging block.

400.description: Bad request — the query parameters or request body are invalid. For example, a queried date range longer than 30 days (`dateFrom`/`dateTo`, or `intervalInHours`/`interval` above 720 hours) returns `status` `FAILURE_BAD_REQUEST` with message `Date range cannot exceed 30 days`.

401.description: Authentication failed, or the authenticated user is not authorized. The Audit API returns 401 (not 403) when the user lacks the Tenant Auditor role — check `status` in the response body to distinguish an invalid token from a missing role.

post.description:
Retrieves audit events for the tenant associated with the authenticated user using a JSON request body to specify the time range. Use `from`/`to` for a specific date range, or `interval` for a rolling window in hours. Use `type` query parameter to filter by event category.

Set `Accept: text/plain` to receive results in Common Event Format (CEF). Default is `application/json`.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Type of events to return. Same vocabulary as the `type` filter on `GET /events` (each value matches an `eventTypeId` prefix): `all`, `user_activity`, `access_control`, `int`, `conn_mgmt`, `comment`, `byok`, `plan_iq`, `forecaster`. The values were confirmed against `GET /events`; the enum is not enforced server-side.

schema.description: Audit events in Common Event Format (CEF), one event per line. Each line has the form `<ISO-8601 timestamp>  CEF:0|Anaplan, Inc.||null|<eventTypeId>|<message>|<key=value extensions>`, where the extension carries the event fields (`id`, `userId`, `tenantId`, `success`, `additionalAttributes`, ...). Unlike the JSON response, the CEF output is not paginated: it returns every event in the requested window, ignores `limit`/`offset`, and has no `meta`/paging block.

400.description: Bad request — the query parameters or request body are invalid. For example, a queried date range longer than 30 days (`dateFrom`/`dateTo`, or `intervalInHours`/`interval` above 720 hours) returns `status` `FAILURE_BAD_REQUEST` with message `Date range cannot exceed 30 days`.

401.description: Authentication failed, or the authenticated user is not authorized. The Audit API returns 401 (not 403) when the user lacks the Tenant Auditor role — check `status` in the response body to distinguish an invalid token from a missing role.

objectId.description: ID of the object acted upon, if applicable.

objectTypeId.description: Type of the object acted upon, if applicable.

objectTenantId.description: Tenant ID of the object acted upon.

additionalAttributes.description: Event-type-specific attributes as a free-form map. Keys vary by event type (for example, `clientName` on token events).

errorNumber.description: Error code, if the action failed.

ipAddress.description: IP address from which the action was performed.

hostName.description: Hostname of the server that recorded the event.

serviceVersion.description: Version of the Anaplan service that generated the event.

eventDate.description: Timestamp when the event occurred (Unix milliseconds, UTC).

createdDate.description: Timestamp when the event was stored in the audit database (Unix milliseconds, UTC).

checksum.description: Checksum for verifying event record integrity. Returned as a numeric string (live testing showed a short integer value, not a SHA-256 hash).

AuditPaging.description: Pagination metadata included in audit event list responses.

totalSize.description: Total number of results matching the query.

offSet.description: Zero-based index of the first result in this page.

nextOffset.description: Offset to use to retrieve the next page, if more results exist.

previousUrl.description: URL to call to retrieve the previous page of results. Present once paging past the first page (offset > 0).

AuditEventsResponse.description: Response envelope for audit event list and search endpoints.

AuditErrorResponse.description: Error response returned by the Audit API. The `status` field identifies the error type. `FAILURE_UNAUTHORIZED_USER_ACTION` indicates the token was accepted but the user lacks the Tenant Auditor role (returned as 401, not 403).

AuditSearchRequest.description: Request body for the POST /events/search endpoint. Specify `from`/`to` for a date range or `interval` for a rolling window. If `to` is omitted, results up to the current time are returned. If `from` is omitted, results from the earliest available event are returned.

from.description: Start of the event date range (Unix timestamp in milliseconds, UTC).

to.description: End of the event date range (Unix timestamp in milliseconds, UTC).

interval.description: Return events from the previous N hours.
